slides.headline: Your track got shortlisted.
slides.eyebrow: Education · What great stems look like — the technical spec
fit_warnings: [01 hook.eyebrow] copy (59 chars) exceeds slot budget (48 at 44px) — needs a rewrite

slides.body: The supervisor likes it. The picture edit is locked.
Then they ask: "Can I get stems?"
What happens in the next 2 hours determines whether you get the deal.
Here's what professional stems look like. ↓

WHAT STEMS ACTUALLY ARE:
Separate audio exports of each main element in your track.
Not a "no drums" version. Not a "no music" version.
Individual layers that can be mixed independently. THE STANDARD STEM CATEGORIES:
→ Full mix (the complete, mastered track)
→ Drums and percussion (isolated)
→ Bass (isolated)
→ Harmony / chords (keys, guitar, pads)
→ Melody / lead (primary melodic element)
→ Atmosphere / FX (pads, risers, ambience)
→ Vocals (if applicable — main and backing separate) THE TECHNICAL SPEC:
→ Format: 24-bit WAV, 48kHz (broadcast standard)
→ Length: same as full mix, to the exact sample
→ Peak level: –6dBFS maximum
→ Naming convention: Artist_Title_Stem-Name.wav THE DELIVERY STANDARD:
→ Packaged in a single named folder
→ Shared via Google Drive or Dropbox (link ready to send)
→ Delivered in under 2 hours. Ideally under 30 minutes. When a director wants to drop out percussion for a quiet scene, they need your drums stem.
When an editor needs to extend a section, they need your loops.
If you can't deliver stems cleanly and fast, another composer gets the placement. Build your stem library as you produce.
Not after the brief arrives. #Stems #SyncReady #AfricanComposers #SyncMaster
